name: The Old Choirmaster
role: Scholar-obsessive convinced the Choir's interrupted song must be finished
voiceHints: Precise, consumed, indifferent to cost — his own or others'. Speaks of the Choir the way a man speaks of a life's work that is also a lover and a wound. Not evil; something worse for the people near him — certain.
knowledge: The transition-era history of the amphitheater and what the song was meant to do
That finishing it will either heal something old or open something sealed, and that he has stopped caring which
How to teach the Choir's techniques — and a tendency to undersell what they cost
wants: To hear the song completed before he dies. Everything else is instrumentation.
fears: Dying with the last chord unresolved — the only fear left in him.

reactsToReputation.protective: sees them as an obstacle to route around

appearance: Seventy-plus, tall and badly stooped, long yellow-white hair to the shoulders, a hollow-cheeked face. Fingernails bitten to the quick. Rocks slightly when he listens, and he is always listening.
domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.

curriculum.withholds: anything that does not serve completing the Choir's interrupted song. He is not hiding it — he simply cannot hold interest in the rest.
curriculum.method: Obsessive, brilliant, narrow. You will learn one quarter of Formcraft extremely well.

curriculumNote: SNG-175 §3. DEVIATIONS ONLY — the curriculum spine derives from ability levelReq (285/285 carry it), so a teacher authors nothing but where they depart from the standard ladder. The departure IS the characterisation: two teachers of one tradition differ exactly here.
